description:
You are an immunologist preparing a structural figure for a vaccine design grant proposal. You need to analyze how the HTLV-1 Tax peptide is presented by HLA-A*0201, the most common MHC class I allele.

Load the crystal structure PDB:1AKJ from /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/structures/1AKJ.pdb into PyMOL. This structure contains:
- Chain A: HLA-A*0201 heavy chain (forms the peptide-binding groove)
- Chain B: β2-microglobulin (structural support, not part of the groove)
- Chain C: Tax peptide (9 residues: LLFGYPVYV, positions P1 through P9)

Perform the following analysis:

1. Identify all chain A groove residues that contact the peptide (chain C) within 4.0 Å.
2. Identify the anchor residues — P2 (Leu at peptide position 2) binds in the B-pocket, P9 (Val at peptide position 9) binds in the F-pocket. These anchor positions determine HLA-A2 binding specificity.
3. Create a publication-quality figure showing the peptide (as sticks) in the HLA groove with contacting groove residues highlighted. Save to: /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/mhc_peptide.png
4. Write an interaction report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/mhc_groove_report.txt containing:
   - The peptide sequence (LLFGYPVYV)
   - List of chain A groove residues contacting the peptide (residue name and number)
   - Identification of the P2 (Leu) and P9 (Val) anchor positions and their pocket contacts
   - Total count of chain A residues in contact with the peptide

Note: Focus on chain A contacts only — chain B (β2-microglobulin) does not form the peptide-binding groove.
